WHAT IS DISENGINEERING?

The Disengineering Society is a “hardware hacking” collective.

ABOUT

We run workshops teaching people how to build electronic musical instruments using salvaged electronic waste.

WORKSHOPS

We also create, publish, and exhibit illustrated educational materials.

SEE OUR ZINES

And host the occasional concert.

CHECK IT OUT

Disengineering is committed to sustainable design practices by combatting planned obsolescence and advocating for Right-to-Repair.

LEARN MORE

OSCILLISCOPE

EVENTS

UPCOMING

WORKSHOP NYC
Tuesday April 14th

The Beep Phone

Tuesday, 14 April 2026

14:00 – 18:00 (GMT-4)

@ Under Computer 610 Manhattan Ave.

Practice creative reuse by turning an old phone into a microphone and its keypad sounds back into a synthesizer in this beginner-friendly Disengineering workshop.

LEARN MORE

PAST EVENTS

WORKSHOP NYC
Wednesday March 25th

Screambox Distortion Amp

Wednesday, 25 March 2026

14:00 – 17:00 (GMT-4)

@ P.I.T. 411 S 5th Street, Brooklyn

Build a gnarly distortion box from salvaged parts in this hands-on session. No prior electronics experience required.

Tuesday June 10th

Vape Mic

Tuesday, 10 June 2025

15:00 – 18:00 (GMT-4)

@ Index Greenpoint 698 Manhattan Ave.

A past workshop on repurposing small electronics into playable microphones.
